series_metadata.series_description: complaints lodged against personnel that are resolved without an internal investigation

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Resolution PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: investigation reports, disciplinary actions, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete THEN: Transfer to official personnel record

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning approved requests for educational leave

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Employee returns or separates THEN: Transfer to official personnel record

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning approved requests for leave under FMLA
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority 29 CFR 825.110 Retention 25 NCAC 01E .0211 25 NCAC 01E .0315 29 CFR 825.500(b)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Employee returns or separates PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning approved leave requests by agency personnel; includes civil leave, community service leave, and leave without pay

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Employee returns or separates PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning approved requests for military leave provided under the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Act (USERRA)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Employee returns or separates PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning approved requests for parental leave

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Employee returns or separates THEN: Transfer to official personnel record

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning participation in the voluntary shared leave program

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning daily hours worked; includes time sheets or time cards
series_metadata.legal_citation: Retention 04 NCAC 24D .0501(a) 29 CFR 516.6(a)(1) NOTE: Time sheets that contain only information necessary for payroll purposes are public records, while those that also include personnel information and/or PII may be in part confidential. 1

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning shift and duty assignments

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: notifications to employees or dependents informing them of their rights to continue insurance coverage after termination or during disability or family leave (e.g., Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act [COBRA])

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Employee returns or eligibility expires PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: death claims filed with the Department of State Treasurer by dependents of retired or active employees

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Settled PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning tuition assistance and reimbursement

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Reimbursement PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: enrollment forms for the State Health Plan received by the Department of State Treasurer; also includes member authorizations and other related records subject to HIPAA compliance

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 6 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_title: Insurance and Fringe Benefits Plans and Programs
series_metadata.series_description: records describing health, life, and other insurance and fringe benefit plans and programs available to agency employees

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_title: Insurance and Fringe Benefits Plans and Programs
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning payment of deferred compensation

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Payment PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Insurance and Fringe Benefits Plans and Programs
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning employer-sponsored retirement plans (e.g., 401(k)); includes plan documents and amendments, trust records, annuity contracts, participant records, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Payment PLUS: 7 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Insurance and Fringe Benefits Plans and Programs
series_metadata.series_description: retirement systems member file maintained by Department of State Treasurer; includes documentation of contributions and remittances, applications for retirement benefits or for return of accumulated retirement contributions, forms identifying beneficiaries for death benefits, correspondence, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Member joins PLUS: 100 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: forms submitted to the Department of Commerce to report wage records of terminated employees SEE ALSO: Unemployment Insurance Records (PUBLIC ASSISTANCE AND SUPPORT SERVICES)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning aptitude and skills tests required of job applicants or of current employees seeking promotion or transfer

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning certification or qualification required for employment, continued employment, or promotion

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Expiration or employee separation PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning pre-employment or periodic records checks conducted on prospective or current staff, interns, and volunteers
series_metadata.legal_citation: NOTE: specific citation for criminal record checks can be found in agency’s enabling legislation

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: includes statements informing employee of the time, place, and reasons for the test; copy of notice sent to examiner identifying employee to be tested; and copies of opinions, reports, or similar records generated by the examiner and provided to the agency

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy NOTE: If no examination is given, destroy records 3 years from date test was requested

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning employees’ work plans; includes goals, tasks, values, and performance evaluations
series_metadata.legal_citation: Confidentiality G.S. § 124-24 25 NCAC 01O .0113(a) Retention 25 NCAC 01O .0113(d) NOTE: Only performance evaluations are confidential

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning employees receiving awards

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: inquiries and responses concerning verification of an employee’s prior or current employment with the agency

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: salary ranges and classifications of positions established by the Office of State Human Resources; includes wage rate tables

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll; includes tax withholding forms (e.g., NC-4, W-4) SEE ALSO: Tax Forms (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Termination of deduction PLUS: 4 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: includes deductions for retirement contributions, bank payments, savings plans, insurance, dues, and other related records SEE ALSO: Tax Forms (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Termination of deduction PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the garnishments of employees’ wages

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Termination of deduction PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning lapsed salaries

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning employees eligible for longevity pay

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning salaries paid to employees (including employed apprentices or students, interns, contract employees, and temporary employees). Contains information such as the name, Social Security number, number of hours worked, compensation rate, deductions, total wages paid, and employer contributions (e.g., retirement, healthcare) paid to each employee per payroll period SEE ALSO: Electronic Funds Transfers (FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT)
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority 29 CFR 516.30(a) Confidentiality G.S. § 132-1.10 2Retention 04 NCAC 24D .0501(a) 29 CFR 516.5(a) 29 CFR 1627.3(a)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Paid PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: notifications of salary changes; includes increments and salary increases mandated by legislative action

retention_rules.disposition: TRANSFER to Personnel File NOTE: Records documenting current and past salaries should be retained in accordance with the public information provision delineated in G.S. § 126-23.

series_metadata.series_description: authorizations and other related records concerning employees receiving shift premium pay

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Paid PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: forms used to determine the gross pay an employee would have earned during a specified period for back pay in a grievance decision, settlement agreement, or a State Human Resources Commission order

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Paid PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: agency personnel policies and procedures SEE ALSO: (below) Workers’ Compensation Program Administration

retention_rules.disposition: PERMANENT

series_metadata.series_description: personnel correspondence and memoranda SEE ALSO: (below) Workers’ Compensation Program Administration

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: agreements and authorizations required of employees; includes orientation materials and informational data SEE ALSO: (below) Workers’ Compensation Program Administration

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: complete history of each employee’s service with the agency

retention_rules.disposition: TRANSFER to Personnel File

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning participants in internships and volunteer positions; includes applications and selection materials as well as aggregated data SEE ALSO: (below) Employee Training Materials (continued on following page)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning participants in apprenticeships, including applications and selection materials as well as aggregated data; also includes affirmative action plans for apprenticeship programs
series_metadata.legal_citation: Authority 29 CFR 30.4(a) 29 CFR 1602.20 Retention 29 CFR 30.12(d) 29 CFR 1602.21

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning discrimination complaints by employees or former employees; includes equal opportunity (EO) complaints SEE ALSO: (below) Applications for Employment

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Final disposition of the charge or action PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy Note: 29 CFR 1602.14 defines final disposition of the charge or the action as “the date of expiration of the statutory period within which the aggrieved person may bring an action in a U.S. District Court or, where an action is brought against an employer either by the aggrieved person, the Commission, or by the Attorney General, the date on which such litigation is terminated.”

series_metadata.series_description: requests for reasonable accommodation by employees or former employees SEE ALSO: (below) Applications for Employment

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Obsolete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_title: Civil Rights Records SEE ALSO (above); Reasonable Accommodations (RISK MANAGEMENT) : Civil Rights Cases
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning documentation of policies and procedures to comply with the Age Discrimination in Employment Act (ADEA), the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), the Equal Pay Act, the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act (GINA), Section 504 of the 1973 Rehabilitation Act, and the 1964 Civil Rights Act; includes reports required by the Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Commission and affirmative action plans

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning disciplinary actions taken against employees SEE ALSO: (below); OAH Case Records, Discrimination Complaint Investigations (LEGAL) Grievances

retention_rules.disposition: TRANSFER to Personnel File NOTE: Records documenting disciplinary actions should be retained in accordance with the public information provision delineated in G.S. § 126-23

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning employees engaging in dual employment with State government or secondary employment with an outside employer

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Termination of outside employment PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: first aid records of minor job-related injuries SEE ALSO: Workers’ Compensation Program Claims (LEGAL)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: physical examinations required by the employer in connection with any personnel action; includes health or physical examination reports, or certificates created in accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) SEE ALSO: Workers’ Compensation Program Claims (LEGAL)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning adopted suggestions of employees through the Employee Suggestion (ES) System of the Department of Administration

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 4 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: employee surveys, rejected suggestions, and other related records outside of the ES System

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_title: Employment Eligibility Verification (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: employment authorization documents filed with the U.S. Department of Labor

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_title: Employment Eligibility Verification (continued on following page)
series_metadata.series_description: E-Verify forms

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Employee separation PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: interviews or questionnaires conducted with employees planning to separate from the agency

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning employee grievances; includes EEO informal inquiry process and formal internal grievance process SEE ALSO: OAH Case Records (LEGAL)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Resolution PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning hearings conducted by the Office of State Human Resources SEE ALSO: OAH Case Records (LEGAL)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Resolution PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_title: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) Records
series_metadata.series_description: records concerning injury or illness, extent and outcomes, summary totals for calendar year, and OSHA forms; also includes ergonomic assessments for employees as well as OSHA inspections and/or citations of the agency NOTE: The receiving agency is the records owner for records generated by Department of Labor assessments

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning personnel action changes (e.g., hire, termination, retirement, resignation)

retention_rules.disposition: TRANSFER to Personnel File NOTE: Records documenting personnel action requests should be retained in accordance with the public information provision delineated in G.S. § 126-23

series_metadata.series_description: records that document events in permanent and temporary individuals’ employment history that have long-term consequences for the employee and the agency; also includes dismissals that are not challenged by the employee

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Separation PLUS: 30 years ΩTHEN: Destroy NOTE: Records should be retained in accordance with the public information provision delineated in G.S. § 126-23

series_metadata.series_description: records that document events in an individual’s short-term employment history

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Separation PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: includes program policies, guidelines, and other related records SEE ALSO: Workers’ Compensation Program Claims (LEGAL)

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning positions that have been abolished

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: statistical data retained by the Office of State Human Resources concerning all applicants who apply for agency positions

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: applications and other related records for hired candidates; includes offers of employment

retention_rules.disposition: TRANSFER to or upon hiring Personnel FileSeasonal and Contract Worker Records

series_metadata.series_description: applications and other related records that are unsolicited or are received from individuals who are not hired or do not accept offered position

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Received PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: includes interview documentation, rosters, eligibility lists, test ranking sheets, tracking forms, justification statements, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Hiring decision PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy*

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning personnel actions and position control, status of each established permanent, temporary full-time, or part-time position, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: job descriptions for each position

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: forms used to evaluate the primary purpose of a position

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: classification records and complete histories of salaried positions within the agency; includes listings providing classification, titles, and position numbers

retention_rules.disposition: PERMANENT

series_metadata.series_description: includes ads and notices of overtime, promotion, and training; also includes employment listings

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: employee-specific records concerning training programs for the proper management of asbestos

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Separation PLUS: 1 year THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning training programs for the proper avoidance of bloodborne pathogen exposure; includes rosters, contents or summaries of sessions, names and qualifications of instructors, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 3 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning the delivery of training to agency personnel; includes training manuals, syllabi and course outlines, and other related records SEE ALSO: (above), (below), and (below) Bloodborne Pathogen TrainingHazardous Waste TrainingLaw Enforcement Training

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Superseded/Obsolete THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: employee-specific records documenting the training of agency personnel; includes certificates, transcripts, test scores, selections, and other related records SEE ALSO: (above) Asbestos Training

retention_rules.disposition: TRANSFER to if training is necessary for current position or could affect career advancement Personnel JacketRETAIN REMAINING RECORDS UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 2 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning training programs for the proper avoidance of hazardous material exposure; includes rosters, contents or summaries of sessions, names and qualifications of instructors, and other related records

retention_rules.disposition: RETAIN UNTIL: Complete PLUS: 5 years THEN: Destroy

series_metadata.series_description: records concerning internal training for law enforcement personnel

retention_rules.disposition: PERMANENT
